text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
The Kikuka Sho, the final race of the Classic
Triple Crown, is before us. The crowd roars in
anticipation of the drama that will unfold.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
Symboli Rudolf, meanwhile, merely waits in
silence.

text_block_list.name: Symboli Rudolf
text_block_list.text:
Conventional wisdom holds that the Kikuka Sho
goes to the strongest Umamusume.

text_block_list.name: Symboli Rudolf
text_block_list.text:
A 3,000-meter course with two uphill stretches is
apt to test any racer's mental fortitude.

text_block_list.name: Symboli Rudolf
text_block_list.text:
It demands speed, stamina, and technique in
equal measure. Only an Umamusume who excels
in every area can hope to win the day.

text_block_list.name: Symboli Rudolf
text_block_list.text:
If I were to cede that crown to another, I could
hardly presume to call myself Emperor.

text_block_list.name: Symboli Rudolf
text_block_list.text:
But as thanks for your faith in me, allow me to be
your escort.

text_block_list.name: Symboli Rudolf
text_block_list.text:
Come with me, Trainer... and we shall see the
highest of heights together.
